key_observations: Strong volume progression from 6.3km (week of 06-08) to 19.3km peak (week of 06-29), demonstrating 206% increase in capacity over 3 weeks
Rising average HR trend (158→160→164→169 bpm) indicates increasing cardiovascular demand; athlete running harder at similar paces, suggesting improved fitness but also accumulating fatigue
Recent taper (10km week of 07-13) combined with highest HR effort (169 bpm avg) signals intentional or unintended recovery week after 2-week build, typical pre-event pattern
warnings: HR climbing 2.75 bpm/week while maintaining/increasing intensity may indicate sympathetic nervous system stress; watch for sleep quality and recovery metrics
Only 1 run in final week (07-13) breaks established 2-3 run pattern; verify if intentional taper or injury/illness related
Missing HR data for first 3 weeks prevents early fatigue detection; ensure HR monitor is consistently worn for accurate monitoring
recommendations: Implement 1-2 easy/recovery runs (Zone 2, sub-150 bpm) next week to balance the high-intensity focus and manage rising acute:chronic ratio above 1.15 threshold
Schedule a deload week (40-50% volume reduction) within next 7-10 days if peak race/event isn't imminent; current trajectory unsustainable without recovery
Establish consistent HR tracking across all runs immediately to catch early overtraining signals; the 11 bpm rise in 3 weeks warrants closer weekly monitoring

weeks_analysis.assessment: Single run baseline; moderate volume, easy pace (6:11/km). Insufficient data for HR assessment.
Volume declined 24%; maintained easy pace (6:12/km). Possible unplanned recovery or reduced availability.
Lowest volume period; pace improved (5:47/km) but single run suggests minimal commitment. Trough before buildup.
Major volume jump (+149%); 3-run structure established; HR baseline 158 bpm indicates controlled aerobic work despite increased frequency.
Peak volume week; maintained 3-run frequency with 23% km increase; HR stable at 160 bpm suggests good adaptation to load.
Reduced to 2 runs (-30% volume) but HR increased 4 bpm; harder individual efforts (175.7 bpm peak run 07-06) indicate quality-focused work or emerging fatigue.
Single long run (10km) at highest average HR (169 bpm); intentional taper or recovery week. Monitor closely for overreaching if not planned.
